evidences.title: Pair: #NMR/USDT Timeframe: Monthly candles (1M) Change: | Crypto_MOON1 on Binance Square
evidences.content: # Pair: #NMR/USDT. Change: +154% (strong bullish monthly move). The price has been in a long downtrend since 2021. A falling wedge has formed, which is usually a bullish reversal pattern. Strong long-term support around $11.80 – $12.00 (price bounced here multiple times). Current candle shows a big bullish breakout from the wedge. This indicates buyers are stepping in strongly. Next resistance zone: $40 – $60. Major resistance: $100 (previous high). NMR has been consolidating for years, but this strong breakout above $20 signals a potential trend reversal. If momentum continues, targets could be $40+ in the mid-term, while $12 remains the key support. Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. Make the reply a new post. evidences.title: How to set the number of klines data each request from Binance
evidences.content: ## Your question Hi, I am trying to run in mode and my strategy is a by-minute one. By default, the number of klines data each request from Binance is 1000, which may excess the rate limit of Binance if keep requesting. I got the warnings: \_async\_get\_candle\_history() returned exception: "Could not fetch historical candle (OHLCV) data for pair BTC/USDT due to ExchangeNotAvailable. Message: binance GET " and error later on: freqtrade.exceptions.DDosProtection: binance {"code":-2015,"msg":"Invalid API-key, IP, or permissions for action."} Could you please elaborate how to set the number of data each request (1000 by default) ? Btw I read from instruction to set rateLimit to 3100. Would that also be helpful? evidences.content: # 🚨 Bitcoin ETFs Are Changing the Game — But at What Cost? Wall Street’s obsession with Bitcoin is shattering records — BlackRock’s IBIT just became the fastest-growing ETF in history, rocketing past $80 billion in assets. Strap in — the market’s about to move. Before you dive into the charts and chase green candles, understand this: Trading isn't just about profits — it’s about survival. You’re probably losing money on trading fees without realizing it — but not anymore! 3. Use Limit Orders Over Market Orders 💰 Limit Orders = Maker fees (often cheaper) 💸 Market Orders = Taker fees (usually higher) > Planning your trades = more profits, fewer surprises. Use Binance’s fee calculator before you confirm trades to preview the cost!
